# UC-15.1.9 · Rack Power Density Trending

## Description

Watts per rack unit over time for capacity planning; identifies hot spots and supports safe equipment placement decisions.

## Value

Watts per rack unit over time for capacity planning; identifies hot spots and supports safe equipment placement decisions.

## Implementation

Aggregate PDU power per rack. Join with DCIM lookup for rack U height. Calculate watts/U. Poll daily or hourly. Alert when watts/U exceeds design threshold (e.g., 500W/U). Use prediction for capacity planning. Report top-density racks and trend by zone.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: SNMP modular input, DCIM integration.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: PDU per-rack power readings, DCIM inventory (rack height, location).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Aggregate PDU power per rack. Join with DCIM lookup for rack U height. Calculate watts/U. Poll daily or hourly. Alert when watts/U exceeds design threshold (e.g., 500W/U). Use prediction for capacity planning. Report top-density racks and trend by zone.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="pdu:rack_power"
| lookup rack_inventory rack_id OUTPUT rack_u_height
| eval watts_per_u=round(power_watts/rack_u_height,1)
| timechart span=1d avg(watts_per_u) as avg_watts_per_u by rack_id
| predict avg_watts_per_u as predicted future_timespan=30
```

Understanding this SPL

**Rack Power Density Trending** — Watts per rack unit over time for capacity planning; identifies hot spots and supports safe equipment placement decisions.

Documented **Data sources**: PDU per-rack power readings, DCIM inventory (rack height, location). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): SNMP modular input, DCIM integration. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: pdu:rack_power. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="pdu:rack_power". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **watts_per_u** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets with a separate series **by rack_id** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Pipeline stage (see **Rack Power Density Trending**): predict avg_watts_per_u as predicted future_timespan=30


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (watts/U trend by rack), Heatmap (rack × density), Bar chart (top racks by density), Table (capacity forecast).

## SPL

```spl
index=power sourcetype="pdu:rack_power"
| lookup rack_inventory rack_id OUTPUT rack_u_height
| eval watts_per_u=round(power_watts/rack_u_height,1)
| timechart span=1d avg(watts_per_u) as avg_watts_per_u by rack_id
| predict avg_watts_per_u as predicted future_timespan=30
```

## Visualization

Line chart (watts/U trend by rack), Heatmap (rack × density), Bar chart (top racks by density), Table (capacity forecast).
